name: india-offshore-wind
note: Indicative offshore wind reference layer for India. Zone polygons are APPROXIMATE extents of the NIWE/FOWIND-identified offshore zones (Gulf of Khambhat off Gujarat; Gulf of Mannar and off Kanyakumari, Tamil Nadu), drawn for orientation only — not survey boundaries or lease blocks. Project/LiDAR points are approximate. Verify against MNRE / NIWE before any commercial use.

policy.label: National Offshore Wind Energy Policy
policy.detail: Framework for offshore wind development within India's Exclusive Economic Zone (up to 200 nautical miles). MNRE is the nodal ministry; NIWE is the nodal agency for resource assessment, surveys and block allocation.
policy.source_name: MNRE — National Offshore Wind Energy Policy, 2015

policy.label: Capacity target
policy.detail: MNRE announced the 30 GW-by-2030 medium-term target (and an interim 5 GW-by-2022 milestone) in 2018, under the framework of the October 2015 National Offshore Wind Energy Policy. The 5 GW-by-2022 milestone was missed — India had no operational offshore capacity by 2022.

policy.label: Viability Gap Funding scheme
policy.detail: Union Cabinet approved (19 Jun 2024) a ₹7,453 cr outlay: ₹6,853 cr VGF to establish 1 GW of offshore wind (500 MW each off Gujarat and Tamil Nadu) plus ₹600 cr for port upgrades. SECI runs the procurement/bidding as implementing agency under MNRE/NIWE oversight; VGF lowers the per-unit tariff for distribution companies.
policy.source_name: PIB / MNRE — Offshore Wind VGF Scheme, 2024

policy.label: Identified resource potential
policy.detail: Preliminary NIWE/FOWIND assessment: ~36 GW off the Gujarat coast and ~35 GW off Tamil Nadu — gross theoretical potential (not net developable capacity), based on mesoscale modelling and early LiDAR campaigns.

policy.label: Seabed lease & block allocation
policy.detail: MNRE strategy paper for allocating offshore wind energy blocks within the EEZ, distinguishing studied areas (allocated on a VGF/competitive basis) from un-studied areas (allocated for survey/study via lease).
policy.source_name: MNRE — Strategy for offshore wind block allocation

properties.name: Gulf of Khambhat zone
properties.note: Off the south Saurashtra coast (Pipavav–Diu). India's first offshore LiDAR campaign sits within this zone.

properties.name: Gulf of Mannar zone
properties.note: Between the Tuticorin–Rameswaram coast and Sri Lanka; part of the ~35 GW Tamil Nadu potential.

properties.name: Off Kanyakumari zone
properties.note: South of Cape Comorin — among India's strongest offshore wind regimes during the south-west monsoon jet.

properties.name: Gujarat 500 MW (VGF Tranche-I)
properties.note: One of the two initial 500 MW blocks under the 2024 VGF scheme; SECI implementing.

properties.name: Gulf of Khambhat LiDAR
properties.note: India's first offshore wind LiDAR, ~25 km off the Gujarat coast (FOWIND).

properties.name: Tamil Nadu 500 MW (VGF Tranche-I)
properties.note: Second of the two initial 500 MW blocks under the 2024 VGF scheme; SECI implementing.

properties.name: Gulf of Mannar LiDAR
properties.note: NIWE offshore LiDAR campaign off the Tamil Nadu coast.
